title: strychnos jobertiana
intro: Strychnos jobertiana, also known as Jobert's poison nut or the poison nut tree, is a plant species in the family Loganiaceae, native to Madagascar. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, as well as being used as a treatment for snake bites and insect stings. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves and bark are steeped in hot water to create a herbal tea.
